story.iWant: execute specific brainstorming techniques (like SCAMPER) via the chat interface
story.soThat: I can generate structured ideas using proven creative thinking methods
story.tasks: - [ ] Task 1: Add Object Type Support to update-variable-tool (NEW PRIMITIVE) - [ ] Task 2: Implement generateInitialMessage Feature for ask-user-chat (NEW PRIMITIVE) - [ ] Task 3: Seed 5 Brainstorming Technique Workflows (AC: 1) - [ ] Task 4: Configure Brainstorming Step 2 with Invoke-Workflow Logic (AC: 2) - [ ] Task 5: Implement Invoke-Workflow Step Type Handler (AC: 2, 4) - [ ] Task 6: Build Action List UI Component (AC: 3) - [ ] Task 7: Build Dialog Layout & Timeline UI Components (AC: 4) - [ ] Task 8: Data Aggregation & State Management (AC: 5) - [ ] Task 9: Integration & End-to-End Testing (AC: All)

story-context.acceptanceCriteria: 1. **Seed Techniques:** Insert 10 technique workflows (e.g., SCAMPER, Six Hats, Tree of Thoughts) into `workflows` table with `tags->>'type' = 'technique'` for filtering 2. **Seed Step 2:** Configure `brainstorming` workflow Step 2 with invoke-workflow logic to execute selected techniques 3. **Action List UI:** Render the list of selected techniques in the chat as an interactive action list (parallel independence pattern) 4. **Child Workflow UI:** Implement Modal/Dialog for running technique workflows with nested conversation interface 5. **Data Aggregation:** Collect outputs from all technique executions into `captured_ideas` variable for next step

doc.title: Product Requirements Document
doc.section: Chat Interface Patterns - Pattern B: Parallel Independence
doc.snippet: Independent items handled in any order with completion state tracking. Visual: REQUIRE ACTION (N) | COMPLETED (N) sections. Use for action lists where user can execute techniques in any order. State tracking allows ignore optional items with feedback.

doc.title: Product Requirements Document
doc.section: Chat Interface Patterns - Pattern D: Focused Dialogs
doc.snippet: Open focused dialog for complex actions while preserving main chat context. Modal/drawer with agent conversation, reasoning, tool calls. Use for technique execution in isolated context. All dialog interactions sync back to main chat timeline.

doc.title: Product Requirements Document
doc.snippet: Guided Not Automated - UI provides structure and clarity while preserving human decision-making at every step. Dialog-Focused Actions - Complex interactions happen in focused dialogs that maintain context while keeping main chat clean.

doc.title: Epic 2: Artifact Workbench
doc.snippet: Implement invoke-workflow step type for child workflow execution. Seed 5 brainstorming techniques (SCAMPER, Six Hats, Five Whys, Mind Mapping, What If Scenarios). Action List UI for parallel technique execution. Dialog layout for focused technique workflow execution.
doc.note: ⚠️ Epic doc mentions "10 techniques" but story corrected to 5 techniques with TypeScript configs in docs/sprint-artifacts/story-2-3-technique-workflows/

doc.title: Architecture Decisions
doc.snippet: Mastra for agent orchestration with ask-user-chat steps. Technique workflows use Mastra agents with generateInitialMessage for dynamic first questions. ACE playbooks for online learning from user feedback. Tools registered with agent for variable updates.

doc.title: Architecture Decisions
doc.snippet: React Query for server state caching with SSE invalidation. Zustand for global UI state and event distribution. Child workflow execution states managed via React Query with real-time updates from invoke-workflow step handler.

doc.title: UX Design Specification
doc.snippet: Modal overlay for complex interactions. Dialog shows agent conversation while parent workflow visible behind with dimmed overlay. Return button closes dialog. Dialog renders step type component (e.g., AskUserChatInterface for ask-user-chat steps).

doc.title: UX Design Specification
doc.section: Pattern 8: Grid Select + List Select Duality
doc.snippet: 3-column icon grid for visual options, vertical list for text-heavy options. Action list pattern for technique execution uses list format with status indicators and execute buttons.

doc.title: Epic 1 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: workflow_executions table tracks execution state with variables (JSONB), executedSteps (JSONB), status enum. Story 2.3 adds parentExecutionId column (nullable UUID FK to self) for child workflow linkage. Timestamps (startedAt, completedAt) track step execution duration.

doc.title: Epic 1 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: All step-specific configuration stored in workflow_steps.config JSONB field. No new tables needed for new step types. Story 2.3 introduces InvokeWorkflowStepConfig and updates AskUserChatStepConfig with generateInitialMessage support.

doc.title: Technique Workflows Pattern Documentation
doc.snippet: Pattern A (Single-Step Sequential): SCAMPER, Six Hats, Five Whys use 1 step with sequential tool unlocking. Pattern B (Multi-Step Phases): Mind Mapping (4 steps), What If Scenarios (3 steps) require different cognitive modes per phase. All techniques use generateInitialMessage: true and access parent variables via {{parent.session_topic}}.

artifact.reason: Story 2.3 Task 2 extends this schema with generateInitialMessage and initialPrompt fields for technique workflows

artifact.symbol: workflowExecutions table, executedSteps JSONB, WorkflowMetadata interface
artifact.reason: Task 5 adds parentExecutionId column and Task 5 Subtask 5.2 adds timestamp recording (startedAt, completedAt) to executedSteps JSONB. Task 3 Subtask 3.1 updates WorkflowMetadata interface to add layoutType field: "wizard" | "artifact-workbench" | "dialog"
Task 1 adds case "object" to switch statement. Currently supports string, number, boolean, enum, array. Need to add object type with valueSchema.properties validation using Zod

Task 2 extends executeStep() to detect generateInitialMessage: true flag and call agent.generate([], { system: resolvedPrompt }) before starting conversation. Need to resolve {{parent.variable}} syntax in initialPrompt
Task 3 follows this pattern to create technique seed files. Same structure: workflow metadata + steps array with step configs. Use for reference when creating scamper.ts, six-thinking-hats.ts, etc.

Task 7 refactors this into ArtifactWorkbenchLayout (one of three layout types). Current implementation has split-pane logic that will be reused. Need to add Timeline container on left pane and make artifact preview self-contained on right pane.
Already includes "invoke-workflow" as option (line 81). No schema change needed, just implement handler in Task 5
Task 5 Subtask 5.10 registers invoke-workflow-handler in this registry. Follow pattern of existing handlers (ask-user-chat-handler, execute-action-handler)

package.name: @mastra/core
package.reason: Agent framework for ask-user-chat steps in technique workflows (Task 3)

package.name: zod
package.reason: Runtime validation for object type schemas in update-variable-tool (Task 1)

package.name: drizzle-orm
package.reason: Database ORM for parentExecutionId column migration (Task 5)

package.name: react
package.reason: UI framework for dialog layout and action list components (Task 6, 7)

package.name: @tanstack/react-query
package.reason: Server state management for child workflow execution states (Task 6, 7)

package.name: lucide-react
package.reason: Icons for action list status indicators and dialog close button

package.name: shadcn/ui
package.reason: Dialog primitives for modal overlay (Task 7 Subtask 7.4)

package.name: bun
package.reason: Test framework for unit tests (Task 1.6, 2.7, 5.12, etc.)

component.name: Step Type Handlers
component.reason: Task 5 introduces invoke-workflow-handler as new step type

component.name: Mastra Agent Tools
component.reason: Task 1 extends update-variable-tool, Task 2 extends ask-user-chat-handler

component.name: Technique Workflows
component.reason: Task 3 seeds 5 technique workflows using existing seeding infrastructure

constraints.constraint: Object validation limited to shallow objects (1 level deep with primitive properties: string, number, boolean, array). Deep nesting, union types, Map/Set types, and schema versioning deferred. Add TODO comments in code marking extension points.

Skip button for action list deferred to future story. Story 2.3 implements [Execute] button only. Completion condition is rigid all-complete (no partial completion or user-confirmed options in this story).
Step 2 is invoke-workflow step type (declarative config), NOT ask-user-chat with tools. No tool called invoke_technique exists. Step handler reads config and creates child executions automatically.

Variable mapping enables selective parent-to-child variable exposure. ONLY variables defined in variableMapping are passed to child executions (no global inheritance). variableMapping provides name translation layer, mapping parent outputVariable names (e.g., "topic") to child expected names (e.g., "session_topic"). Child workflows access mapped variables via {{parent.variable_name}} syntax (e.g., {{parent.session_topic}}).

Dialog layout is intentional cognitive isolation (feature, not bug). Provides zen focus on one technique without parent workflow distraction. User can close dialog and reopen to resume paused child execution.

Parallel execution is optional user behavior, not forced. User can execute techniques sequentially (complete SCAMPER → execute Six Hats) or in parallel (open both dialogs). Progressive disclosure ensures no overwhelming context.
JSONB step config storage pattern continues from Epic 1. No new tables for invoke-workflow step type - uses existing workflow_steps.config JSONB field with InvokeWorkflowStepConfig schema.
Child workflow executions linked via parentExecutionId (new column in workflow_executions table). Nullable UUID FK to workflow_executions.id enables parent-child tree tracking.

5 technique workflows seeded from TypeScript configs in docs/sprint-artifacts/story-2-3-technique-workflows/ (NOT 10 as Epic 2 doc states). Configs follow Chiron seed pattern established in Epic 1 (workflow-init-new, brainstorming).
All technique workflows must have metadata.layoutType = "dialog" to ensure dialog overlay rendering. Tags must include { type: "technique", category: "structured|creative|deep" } for filtering.
Action list renders invoke-workflow step state (child execution status), not tool calls. [Execute] button opens dialog for child workflow execution via UI navigation, not tool invocation.

Layout system uses WorkflowLayoutRenderer to route workflows to correct layout (wizard, artifact-workbench, dialog) based on metadata.layoutType. StepRenderer returns pure step content with no layout concerns. Timeline is specific to artifact-workbench layout only - wizard and dialog layouts render step content directly.

ArtifactWorkbenchLayout is self-contained: receives execution and stepContent, internally renders ArtifactPreview on right pane. No artifactContent prop needed - layout handles artifact rendering from execution state.

Timeline manages focused/browse mode switching. Focused mode renders step content (passed as children) at 100% height with other steps hidden. Browse mode shows accordion of all steps for navigation - clicking accordion item switches to focused mode for that step.

Comprehensive unit tests required for object validation (required fields, type validation, shallow structures). Integration tests for invoke-workflow handler with mocked child executions. End-to-end test: Step 1 (select techniques) → Step 2 (execute 2+ techniques) → verify captured_ideas aggregation.

interface.name: InvokeWorkflowStepConfig
interface.note: Implemented in Task 4 (config) and Task 5 (schema + handler). Added variableMapping after initial planning - maps parent outputVariable names to child expected names. Key = child variable name (what child accesses via {{parent.X}}), Value = parent variable reference (e.g., "{{topic}}").

interface.name: workflow_executions.parentExecutionId
interface.note: Added in Task 5 Subtask 5.1. Enables parent-child workflow tree tracking.

interface.name: executedSteps JSONB Schema
interface.note: Subtask 5.2 adds timestamp recording for timeline browse mode display.

interface.name: UpdateVariableToolConfig (Object Type)
interface.note: Task 1 adds object type support. Subtask 1.6 adds TODOs for nested objects, union types, Map/Set.

interface.name: AskUserChatStepConfig (with generateInitialMessage)
interface.note: Task 2 implements this primitive. All 5 techniques use generateInitialMessage: true.

interface.name: workflows.metadata.layoutType
interface.note: Subtask 3.8 sets layoutType = "dialog" for all technique workflows. Update WorkflowMetadata interface to add layoutType field.

interface.name: StepRenderer
interface.note: Pure step content rendering. Layout wrapper applied by WorkflowLayoutRenderer based on metadata.layoutType.

interface.name: Workflow Layout System
interface.note: Task 7 implements layout system. Timeline is specific to artifact-workbench only. Other layouts render step content directly.

interface.name: ActionListProps
interface.note: Task 6 implements action list. onExecute opens dialog, does NOT call a tool.

interface.name: DialogLayoutProps
interface.note: Task 7 Subtask 7.4 creates dialog-layout. Receives step content from WorkflowLayoutRenderer, does not fetch execution internally.

interface.name: Timeline Component
interface.note: Task 7 Subtasks 7.1-7.3. Timeline wraps step content in focused mode. Browse mode uses accordion for navigation (shadcn accordion component).

tests.standards: Chiron uses Bun test framework for all testing. Follow existing patterns in packages/api/src/services/workflow-engine/step-handlers/*.test.ts and packages/db/src/schema/*.test.ts. Unit tests validate Zod schemas, handler logic, and tool execution. Integration tests use Docker PostgreSQL (not in-memory SQLite). Component tests use React Testing Library. End-to-end tests validate full workflow execution from Step 1 (select techniques) through Step 2 (execute techniques) to verify captured_ideas aggregation.

ideas.test: Verify 5 technique workflows seeded with correct tags (type: "technique") and metadata (layoutType: "dialog"). Query database after seed script runs and assert count = 5 with SCAMPER, Six Hats, Five Whys, Mind Mapping, What If Scenarios present.

Verify brainstorming workflow Step 2 has stepType = "invoke-workflow" with config containing workflowsToInvoke, inheritParentVariables, expectedOutputVariable, aggregateInto, completionCondition fields. Assert no tools array exists in config.

Render ActionList component with mock child executions (2 pending, 1 running, 1 completed). Assert [Execute] buttons present for pending items, running indicator shows for in-progress, checkmark shows for completed. Test onExecute callback triggers on button click.

Render DialogLayout component with mock executionId. Assert modal overlay visible, parent workflow dimmed behind dialog, AskUserChatInterface component renders inside dialog. Test close button pauses child execution and closes dialog.

Integration test: Execute Step 2 with 2 child workflows. Mock child workflow completion with generated_ideas outputs. Verify invoke-workflow handler reads expectedOutputVariable from each child and appends to parent's captured_ideas array. Assert final captured_ideas length = 2 with technique IDs present.

Unit test: buildUpdateVariableTool with valueType: "object" and valueSchema.properties. Test validation of required fields, type checking (string, number, boolean, array properties), and additionalProperties for dynamic keys. Test Five Whys Q&A pair structure and Mind Mapping dynamic branches.

Unit test: AskUserChatHandler.executeStep() with generateInitialMessage: true. Mock agent.generate() call. Assert initialPrompt resolves {{parent.session_topic}} correctly. Verify generated message returned as generated_initial_message in step output.

Unit test: invoke-workflow-handler creates child workflow_executions with parentExecutionId set. Test global state inheritance (entire parent variables JSONB copied to child). Test completion condition checking (all-complete waits for all children). Test output aggregation from children.

Component test: TimelineFocusedView shows only active step at 100% space with child workflows listed. TimelineBrowseView shows all steps with timestamps (startedAt, completedAt) and duration calculation. Test toggle between modes.

E2E test: Complete brainstorming workflow from Step 1 (select SCAMPER + Six Hats) → Step 2 (execute both techniques in parallel) → verify captured_ideas contains ideas from both techniques. Test dialog opens for each technique, user completes SCAMPER, then Six Hats, action list shows both as completed, Step 2 advances after all-complete condition met.
